evals.prompt:
I need you to write a Python function that reads a CSV file and returns the data as a list of dictionaries.

[Agent writes code]

Actually, that's not handling the case where the file doesn't exist. Add error handling for that.
evals.expected_output: A .code-reviews.md file should be created in the repository root with an entry containing: the original code without error handling, the user's feedback about missing file-not-found handling, and the corrected code with proper error handling. The entry should include a timestamp, file path, and properly formatted markdown with code blocks.

assertions.name: has-timestamp-and-filepath
assertions.description: Entry includes timestamp (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM) and file path

assertions.name: has-before-code-section
assertions.description: Entry has '修正前' section with Python code block

assertions.name: has-feedback-section
assertions.description: Entry has '修正指示' section with user feedback

assertions.name: has-after-code-section
assertions.description: Entry has '修正後' section with corrected Python code

evals.prompt:
Can you write a JavaScript function to validate email addresses using regex?

[Agent writes code]

No wait, that regex pattern is too simple and will miss a lot of invalid emails. Use a more comprehensive pattern like this one: /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/
evals.expected_output: The .code-reviews.md file should have a new entry logged with: the original simple regex pattern, the user's specific correction about using a better regex pattern (including their suggested pattern), and the updated code. The entry should be appended to the existing file.

assertions.name: entry-appended
assertions.description: New entry was appended to existing .code-reviews.md

assertions.name: has-timestamp-and-filepath
assertions.description: Entry includes timestamp and file path

assertions.name: has-before-regex
assertions.description: Entry shows original simple regex pattern

assertions.name: has-user-suggested-pattern
assertions.description: User's suggested regex pattern is included in feedback

assertions.name: has-corrected-code
assertions.description: Entry has corrected code with better regex

evals.prompt:
Write a TypeScript function that fetches data from an API endpoint and processes it.

[Agent writes code]

This looks good, but you forgot to add types for the response data. Can you add a proper interface for the API response?
evals.expected_output: A new entry in .code-reviews.md containing: the original untyped function, the user's feedback about missing TypeScript types, and the corrected version with a proper interface. The code blocks should use 'typescript' as the language identifier.

assertions.name: has-untyped-before
assertions.description: Before section shows function without types

assertions.name: has-typed-after
assertions.description: After section shows function with interface and proper types
